title: API Keys
description: Manage authentication for external AI providers and service integrations

sections.title: API Keys
sections.subtitle: Securely connect AgentLog to AI providers and external services
sections.description: API keys let your agents call AI model providers and integrate with third-party services. Every key is encrypted with AES-256-GCM before it is stored, so secrets are never persisted in plaintext.
highlights: 🔌 Integrations: Slack, Discord, GitHub, OpenWeather, Neo4j, Google Drive

sections.title: What are API Keys used for?
sections.content:
API keys authenticate AgentLog with the external systems your agents rely on:

• **AI Providers**: Power model execution (Gemini, OpenAI, OpenRouter)
• **Integrations**: Let functions talk to services like Slack, GitHub, and Google Drive
• **Per-agent overrides**: Assign specific keys to individual agents when needed
• **Encrypted storage**: All keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before being saved

steps.task: Go to the API Keys screen
steps.description: Open the API Keys section from the navigation menu
Pick the AI provider (Gemini, OpenAI, OpenRouter) or integration you want to connect
Copy the secret from the provider's dashboard and paste it into the key field
AgentLog validates the key, encrypts it with AES-256-GCM, and stores it securely
Override the key for specific agents if they should use a different account

sections.title: Best Practices
tips: 🔐 **Use scoped keys**: Grant each key the minimum permissions it needs
♻️ **Rotate regularly**: Replace keys periodically and after any suspected exposure
🧪 **Validate first**: Confirm a key works before assigning it to production agents
🚫 **Never share secrets**: Keys are write-only in the UI and cannot be retrieved after saving
📊 **Monitor usage**: Watch provider dashboards for unexpected activity or quota spikes

solutions: Confirm the key was copied in full with no leading or trailing spaces
Verify the key matches the selected provider or service
Check that the key is active and not expired in the provider's dashboard
Ensure the associated account has billing or quota enabled

problems.issue: Agent execution fails with an auth error
solutions: Verify the provider key is set and valid on the API Keys screen
Check whether the agent has a per-agent key override pointing at a different account
Confirm the key has permission for the model or scope the agent requests
Re-add the integration key (Slack, GitHub, etc.) and re-validate it
Confirm the token's scopes cover the operation the function performs
Regenerate the token in the third-party service and update it in AgentLog

sections.title: Ready to connect your services?
sections.subtitle: Add a key and start running agents

actions.title: Manage API Keys
actions.description: Add or update your provider and integration keys

actions.title: Configure a Model
